THE TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR A MILE OR SO ACROSS CENTRAL HILL TOWNSHIP, MAINLY OVER OPENED FIELDS. IT KICKED UP CONSIDERABLE DUST ACROSS THE SOUTH FLANK OF THE STORM AS INDICATED BY SPOTTER VIDEOS. SOME LARGE BRANCHES WERE REPORTED BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS. THE TORNADO ENDED ABOUT 5 SSW OF BUFFALO BY 404 PM CDT.
SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS ACROSS NORTHERN MORGAN TOWNSHIP.
CALL FROM A SPOTTER WITH A REPORT OF A TORNADO ON THE GROUND

APPARENT THUNDERSTORM DOWNBURST DAMAGE. A LARGE SOUTH FACING SHED DOOR WAS BLOWN IN AND OVER A THIRD OF THE ROOFING WAS PEALED OFF OF A LARGE 40X140 POLESHED. LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN DOWN, A SMALL CAMPER WAS FLIPPED OVER AND A NEARBY SMALL SHED WAS BLOWN DOWN. SHOP ROOFING WAS BLOWN UP TO 300 YARDS TO THE NORTHEAST. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. ADDITIONAL BRANCH DAMAGE WAS NOTED ACROSS SOUTHERN UNION TOWNSHIP.
TREES DOWN IN BUFFALO. 6 TO 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
APPARENT THUNDERSTORM DOWNBURST DAMAGE. SHED ROOFING WAS PEALED UP, LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN DOWN, AND A SMALL CAMPER WAS FLIPPED OVER. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN WAS NOTED OVER AN OPEN FIELD. THE TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD FOR LESS THAN A QUARTER MILE AN LIFTED BY 413 PM CDT. NO DAMAGE WAS NOTED THOUGH SIGNIFICANT DUST WAS LIFTED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 75 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATD TO 75 YARDS.
SHORT LIVED TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR A FEW MINUTES BEFORE LIFTING
TREES DOWN IN BUFFALO. 6 TO 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
TWO TRACTOR TRAILER RIGS WERE FLIPPED TO THE EAST, ON THEIR SIDES, ALONG THE SOUTHBOUND LANE OF INTERSTATE 29, DUE TO DOWNBURST WINDS. OCCURRED BETWEEN 429 AND 431 PM CDT.
TWO SEMIS FLIPPED OVER ON I-29 DUE TO WINDS
FIVE POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED AND THREE WERE LEFT LEANING ALONG A HALF MILE STRETCH OF HIGHWAY 81. NEARBY FARMSTEADS HAD A FEW SNAPPED OR UPROOTED TREES.
VERY STRONG WINDS TORE STEEL ROOFING OFF OF A BARN AND BLEW OUT A WINDOW.

LOTS OF ROTATION WITH THE STORM, SENDING OUT FUNNEL CLOUDS EVERY FEW MINUTES. A COUPLE BRIEF TOUCHDOWNS WERE EVIDENT IN VIDEO OVER AN OPENED FIELD. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 70 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 50 YARDS. TRACK LENGTH AND DURATION LESS THAN ONE QUATER MILE AND LESS THAN A MINUTE.

A TORNADO LIKELY TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO ABOUT 1SW NIAGRA BY 506 PM CDT. A FEW SNAPPED OR UPROOTED POPLAR TREES WERE NOTED IN A SHELTER BELT NORTH OF U.S. HIGHWAY 2, WEST OF MP 239. LARGE BRANCHES WERE ALSO BROKEN DOWN IN VARIOUS SHELTER BELTS WEST OF NIAGRA. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 90 MPH. MAXIMUN WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 150 YARDS. TIMING ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
A FEW LARGE 4 TO 6 INCH DIAMETER TREE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN DOWN BROKEN DOWN IN A FARM YARD. STRONG WINDS BLEW FOR SEVERAL MINUTES.
VERY STRONG WINDS AND WIND DRIVEN HAIL STRIPPED CORN AND BEAN FIELDS ACROSS CENTRAL MAPLE RIVER TOWNSHIP, NEAR LYNCHBURG.
SEVERAL LARGE 4-6 INCH DIAMETER BRANCHES AND LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS IN NORTHEAST TYNSID AND NORTHWEST ROOME TOWNSHIP.

A TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD FROM THE FAR NORTHEAST EDGE OF OAKVILLE TOWNSHIP INTO FAR NORTHWEST BRENNA TOWNSHIP, WITH BRIEF AND INTERMITTENT TOUCHDOWNS ALONG A ROUGHLY MILE LONG PATH... ENDING ABOUT 2 SSW OF THE GRAND FORKS AIRPORT BY 534 PM CDT. AT LEAST ONE POPLAR TREE WAS SNAPPED IN A SHELTER BELT ALONG WITH SEVERAL SMALL WILLOW BUSHES IN A NEARLY HOMESTEAD. A PARTIALLY RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO LIKELY TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY TO THE SOUTHEAST FOR ABOUT 3 MILES, ENDING ABOUT 4 SSW OF AMOURDALE BY 924 PM CDT. AT LEAST ONE POWER POLE WAS CRACKED AND POWER LINES WERE TORN DOWN IN CENTRAL MOUNTAIN VIEW TOWNSHIP. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS. TIME BASED ON RADAR AND SPOTTER REPORTS.
2 HOPPER BOTTOMED GRAIN BINS AND A GRAIN DRYER WERE BLOWN OVER AT A SITE JUST WEST OF THE LAKE. A 5K BUSHEL STEEL GRAIN BIN WAS DENTED IN NEAT THE ROOF LINE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

REPORT OF TREES BLOWN DOWN AROUND TOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. A STORM SURVEY ALSO FOUND THAT SOME STEEL SIDING HAD BEEN TORN OFF THE SIDES OF A GRAIN CLEANING FACILITY.

A PARTIALLY RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO LIKELY TRACKED EASTWARD FOR ROUGHLY 4 MILES ACROSS SOUTHERN VIRGINIA TOWNSHIP, ENDING ABOUT 6 NNW OF EGELAND BY 956 PM CDT. THE TORNADO PICKED AND TOSSED A HOPPER BOTTOM GRAIN BIN ABOUT 40 YARDS TO THE SOUTHWEST, BLEW DOWN CATTLE FENCING AT A FARMSTEAD, AND BROKE DOWN NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES AND LIMBS ALONG ITS PATH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 250 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 90 MPH. TIME BASE ON RADAR.

A PARTIALLY RAIN WRAPPED TORNADO LIKELY TRACKED 3 TO 4 MILES TO THE ESE, FROM THE NORTHWEST CORNER OF CALIO INTO NORTHEAST SEIVERT TOWNSHIP, ENDING ROUGHLY 3 SSW OF MUNICH BY 1033 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BLEW DOWN SEVERAL TREES IN CALIO AND TORE SOME STEEL SHEETS OF A SHOP ROOF. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.

A BOWING SECTION OF A SQUALL LINE APPEARS TO HAVE CUT ACROSS ACROSS EAST CENTRAL AVON TOWNSHIP. EXTREME WIND DAMAGE EXTENDED TO THE NORTH-NORTHEAST TO AROUND 7 MILES SOUTH OF ARVILLA BY 357 AM CDT. LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR SHEARED IN MULTIPLE SHELTER BELTS ALONG ITS PATH.

A BOWING SECTION OF A SQUALL LINE APPEARS TO HAVE SPAWNED TWO BRIEF TORNADOES ACROSS PORTIONS OF AVON TOWNSHIP. THIS FIRST TORNADO TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR ROUGHLY FOUR MILES, AND LIKELY ENDED ABOUT 7 MILES SOUTH OF ARVILLA BY 357 AM CDT. LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR SHEARED IN MULTIPLE SHELTER BELTS ALONG ITS PATH. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 250 YARDS.

AN EMBEDDED MICROBURST INITIATED IN THE BOWING SECTION OF A SQUALL LINE AS IT MOVED INTO AVON TOWNSHIP. EXTREME WIND DAMAGE WAS APPARENT EXTENDING NORTHWARD ACROSS NORTHWEST AVON TOWNSHIP. TREES WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS AND 3 CATTLE TRAILERS WERE OVERTHROWN AT A FARMSTEAD.

A BRIEF TORNADO INITIATED TO THE NORTH OF A BOWING SECTION OF A SQUALL LINE AS IT MOVED INTO AVON TOWNSHIP. EXTREME WIND DAMAGE WAS APPARENT EXTENDING NORTHWARD FOR ROUGHLY 2 MILES, TO AROUND 5SSE OF LARIMORE BY 358 AM CDT. TREES WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS AND 3 CATTLE TRAILERS WERE OVERTHROWN AT A FARMSTEAD. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 200 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO DEVELOP TO THE NORTH OF THE BOWING SECTION OF A SQUALL LINE IN AVON TOWNSHIP. IT APPEARS TO HAVE TRACKED NEARLY DUE NORTH FOR ROUGHLY 3 MILES, ENDING AROUND 6 MILES SOUTHWEST OF ARVILLA BY 359 AM CDT. TREES WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS AND 3 CATTLE TRAILERS WERE OVERTHROWN AT A FARMSTEAD. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTMATED TO 200 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO WAS LIKELY WRAPPED IN DOWNBURST WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN. IT APPEARS TO HAVE TRACKED EASTWARD FOR A MILE OR SO TO ABOUT 1.5 NE OF TWIN VALLEY BY 450 AM CDT. LARGE TREES WERE BROKEN DOWN AND FLUNG IN VARIOUS DIRECTIONS, BUT MAINLY TO THE NORTHEAST AND EAST. METAL ROOFING WAS TORN OFF A SHED. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 90 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 150 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO WAS LIKELY WRAPPED IN DOWNBURST WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN FOR MOST OF ITS PATH. IT TRACKED NEARLY DUE EAST AT ABOUT 60 MPH, FOR AT LEAST 15 MILES, AND ENDED ROUGHLY 2 MILES ESE OF FOSSTON BY 509 PM CDT. IT TORE DOWN TREES IN SHELTER BELTS, TORE ROOFING OFF OF A COUPLE OF HOMES AND FARM BUILDINGS. IT TORE DOWN 11 MILES, OR MORE THAN 50 HIGH VOLTAGE TOWERS, AND SNAPPED WOODEN POWER POLES AT CROSS ROADS ALONG ITS PATH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 400 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 120 MPH.

A TORNADO WHICH WAS PARTIALLY WRAPPED IN DOWNBURST WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN LIKELY TRACKED FOR ROUGHLY 2.5 MLES TO THE EAST AND LIFTED BY 459 PM CDT. THIS TORNADO SNAPPED VARIOUS HARDWOOD AND PINE TREES AT ONE FARMSTEAD. AFTER CROSSING HIGHWAY 2, ROUGHLY 2 MILES ESE OF THE HIGHWAY 59 INTERSECTION, IT BLEW IN A LARGE WEST FACING OVERHEAD DOOR ON A SHOP AND BLEW OUT THE BACK WALL. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 100 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED TO 250 YARDS.

DOWNBURST WINDS HITS HOMESTEADS ACROSS EASTERN COULEE AND WESTERN DRY LAKE TOWNSHIPS. AT ONE HOMESTEAD, WIND OF 70 TO 80 MPH, BLEW IN THE OVERHEAD DOOR OF A SOUTH FACING ATTACHED GARAGE. STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO INTERMITTENTLY TRACKED 26 MILES FROM 3 MILES NNE OF FOSSTON TO 9 MILES NNE OF LEONARD. SEVERAL CABINS AND FARM OUTBUILDINGS AND ONE HOUSE HAD DAMAGED ROOFS, SEVERAL CAMPERS AND BOATS WERE TOSSED AND DAMAGED, CROPS WERE DAMAGED, AND OVER 200 TREES WERE BADLY DAMAGED, UPROOTED, OR HAD TRUNKS SNAPPED.
